title: [InstCombine] Relax the conditons of fold of `ucmp`/`scmp` into phi by allowing the phi node to use the result of `ucmp`/`scmp` more than once
summary:
The generalization transforms a single-use check (`hasOneUse()`) into a single-user check (`hasOneUser()`), enabling the optimization to handle cases where the phi node has multiple incoming edges from the same block (like the test's `switch` with duplicate destinations). This prevents creating invalid phi nodes when merging multiple clones into the same incoming block.

The patch also introduces a `SmallDenseMap` to deduplicate clones per incoming block, ensuring only one clone is created per unique predecessor block, even when multiple phi edges originate from it. This makes the optimization robust for CFGs with multi-edges while remaining profitable.

**Key insight**: When generalizing from a specific test, identify the underlying constraint (here, avoiding duplicate clones per block) and relax the precondition (`hasOneUse` → `hasOneUser`) while adding deduplication logic. This preserves correctness and expands applicability to broader patterns.
